remaining_blockers: Benchmark breadth (W1): need harder real-world polygon dataset OR narrow claim. Out of scope for auto-loop.
Paper writing: claim must stay sharply scoped — 'preprocessing-light arc-token interface', not 'universal accuracy winner'.
key_winning_findings: Arc tokens beat point tokens for 4 of 5 encoders × 3 datasets (Tables 4, 1b, 2b)
PointNet 20w-1s = 0.8989 ± 0.002 vs SketchEmbedNet raw 0.8667 (+3.22 pt over 3 seeds)
75-1000x preprocessing speedup vs Geo2Vec/Poly2Vec with no quality penalty (Table 7)
Reflection failure of arc tokens fully fixed with 1-line augmentation (+57 to +79 pt under reflect_x, Table 8b)
Size-only floor 0.74 buildings / 0.51 mnist; ArcSet +18-47 pt above is real shape encoding
key_honest_limitations: PointNet on raw 2-D points beats PointNet on arc tokens by ~2 pt on buildings_iso (T-Net is point-cloud-native)
Arc tokens reflection-sensitive without augmentation (Table 8 catastrophic failure → 8b verified fix)
Scale invariance comes from preprocessing, not encoder learning
Buildings size-only floor 0.74 indicates the dataset is somewhat aspect-ratio-driven
SEN/Sketchformer are TF2 reimpls, not upstream code
